mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly supporting higher S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels across rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter as corporate revenues accelerate.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, with out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over 20 days when the economy operates above potential output.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or signal accuracy drops below 50% when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.35%, or Rank IC is negative over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent non-farm payroll growth sustains household income and consumer spending trajectories, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon through earnings channel transmission.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction, with cumulative return differential between high and low growth quintiles statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales validates synchronized demand expansion across business and consumer sectors, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market model, with hit rate below 52% across 100+ triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks are followed by positive SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for volatility regimes.
